zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Franklin Lambert於1455年2月11日出生在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾斯吉普頓時，他的父親John Durham Lambert31歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Whitacres25歲。他於1489年與Elva Woolstenhulme在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1532年5月7日在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾斯托克 布盧納享年77歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Lambert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Dutch, German, Polish, Czech, and Slovak: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements land ‘land, territory’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In England, the native Old English form Landbeorht was replaced by Lambert, the Continental form of the name that was taken to England by the Normans from France. The name gained wider currency in Britain in the Middle Ages with the immigration of weavers from Flanders, among whom Saint Lambert or Lamprecht, bishop of Maastricht in around 700, was a popular cult figure. In Italy the name was popularized in the Middle Ages as a result of the fame of Lambert I and II, Dukes of Spoleto and Holy Roman Emperors. Lambert is the second most frequent surname in Wallonia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht (see also Lampert ).

History: Aubin Lambert dit Champagne from Tourouvre in Orne, France, married Élisabeth Aubert in Quebec City, QC, in 1670; see also Champagne . Eustache Lambert from Boulogne-sur-Mer in Pas-de-Calais, France, married Marie Laurence in France c. 1656, died in Quebec City, QC, in 1673. Pierre Lambert from Fourmetot in Eure, France, married Marie Normand in Quebec City, QC, in 1680. Augustin Hébert dit Lambert, a descendant of Augustin Hébert from Paris, France, married Françoise Petit in Varennes, QC, in 1761. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Lambers
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberth
meanings: English: variant of   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lambertus
meanings: German and Dutch: humanistic name, Latinized form of the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gaetano, Luciano, Amedeo, Angelo, Biagio, Domenic, Donato, Enrico, Gino, Iolanda.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Lamberto, an equivalent of Lambert . This surname is also found in Corsica and southern France.
German and Dutch: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  , using the genitive of its Latinized form   Lambertus  .

variants.name: Lamberty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ana, Dimas, Jose, Juan, Altagracia, Angel, Blanca, Efrain, Fernando, Jaime, Ramon.
German and French: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Lambert , from Lamberti, the genitive of its Latinized form Lambertus . Compare Lamberti 2.

variants.name: Lambertz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Erna, Johann, Ralf.
North German: patronymic from   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberto
meanings: Italian and Spanish: from the personal name Lamberto (see Lambert ).

variants.name: Lampert
meanings: English, North German, Dutch, and Slovak; Hungarian (Lampért): variant of Lambert . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed some cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht.
English: variant of Lambard, an occupational name from Middle English lambherd, lambhird, lambhurd ‘lamb-herd’ (Old English lamb + hierde).

variants.name: Lambertsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
Dutch, Danish, North German, and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  . This surname has died out in the Netherlands.
History: Cornelius Lambertsen came from Utrecht, the Netherlands, arriving at New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY) in 1642.

variants.name: Lamb
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Lamb, a pet form of Lambert .
English: nickname for a meek and inoffensive person, from Middle English lamb, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper of lambs. See also Lamm .

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Luain ‘descendant of the warrior’, formerly Anglicized as O'Loan (see Lane 3). MacLysaght comments: "The form Lamb(e), which results from a more than usually absurd pseudo-translation (uan ‘lamb’), is now much more numerous than O'Loan itself.".

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). nameMeanings.name: Franklin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, derived from Middle English frankeleyn ‘freeman’, denoting a member of a class of men who were not of noble birth but who were nevertheless freeholders. The vocabulary word is derived from Old French franc, meaning both ‘free’ and ‘Frankish’. The connection between freemen and Franks is reflected in the Late Latin term francalia, originally denoting lands held by Franks, which came to mean lands not subject to taxes. The given name is now quite common, especially in the United States, where it is often bestowed in honour of the statesman and scientist Benjamin Franklin ( 1706–90 ). A more recent influence was President Franklin D. Roosevelt ( 1882–1945 ).

variants.name: Franklyn
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, derived from Middle English frankeleyn ‘freeman’, denoting a member of a class of men who were not of noble birth but who were nevertheless freeholders. The vocabulary word is derived from Old French franc, meaning both ‘free’ and ‘Frankish’. The connection between freemen and Franks is reflected in the Late Latin term francalia, originally denoting lands held by Franks, which came to mean lands not subject to taxes. The given name is now quite common, especially in the United States, where it is often bestowed in honour of the statesman and scientist Benjamin Franklin ( 1706–90 ). A more recent influence was President Franklin D. Roosevelt ( 1882–1945 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Franklin Lambert was born on 11 February 1455, in Skipton, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Durham Lambert, was 31 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Whitacres, was 25. He married Elva Woolstenhulme in 1489, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 7 May 1532, in Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 77, and was buried in London, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Franklin Lambert est né le 11 février 1455, à Skipton, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Durham Lambert, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Whitacres, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Elva Woolstenhulme en 1489, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 7 mai 1532, à Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterré à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Franklin Lambert am 11. Februar 1455 in Skipton, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Durham Lambert 31 und seine Mutter Lady Elizabeth Whitacres 25. Er hat 1489 in England, United Kingdom Elva Woolstenhulme geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 7. Mai 1532 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in London, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Franklin Lambert è nato il il 11 febbraio 1455 nel Skipton, North Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Durham Lambert, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Lady Elizabeth Whitacres, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Elva Woolstenhulme nel 1489, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 7 maggio 1532, nel Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolto a London, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Franklin Lambertが1455年2月11日にイギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアスキプトンで生まれた時，父John Durham Lambertは31歳，母Lady Elizabeth Whitacresは25歳であった。1489年にイギリスイギリスでElva Woolstenhulmeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1532年5月7日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアストク ブルエルンで77歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Franklin Lambert(이)가 1455년 2월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 스키프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Durham Lambert(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Whitacres(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1489년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Elva Woolstenhulme(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1532년 5월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 스토크 브루어니에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Franklin Lambert nasceu em 11 de fevereiro de 1455, em Skipton, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Durham Lambert, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Whitacres, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Elva Woolstenhulme em 1489, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 7 de maio de 1532, em Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 77 anos, e foi sepultado em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Franklin Lambert родился 11 февраля 1455 г. в местности скиптон, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Durham Lambert было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Whitacres, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Elva Woolstenhulme в 1489 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 7 мая 1532 г. в местности сток бруирн, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Franklin Lambert nació el 11 de febrero de 1455, en Skipton, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Durham Lambert, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Whitacres, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Elva Woolstenhulme en 1489, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 7 de mayo de 1532, en Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultado en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
